description:
You are acting as a release-manager who has to prepare a quick deployment
    summary.  
    A text file already exists at  

        /home/user/releases/services_2023-10.txt  

    containing one micro-service name per line (duplicates allowed).  
    Your goal is to create a frequency report that tells how many times each
    service appears in that list.

    1. Generate the frequency counts with the classic
       “sort | uniq -c” pipeline (feel free to add whatever extra “sort”
       or “awk” steps you need).  
    2. Write the final, human-readable report to a new file, exactly named  

        /home/user/releases/service_frequency.log  

       • Each line of service_frequency.log must follow this precise pattern
         (note the colon, space, and no leading zeroes):  

             <service_name>: <count>

         Example for a hypothetical service named api:  
             api: 7  

       • The lines in service_frequency.log must be ordered:
         a. Descending by the count.  
         b. Alphabetically (ascending) when counts are tied.  
       • Do NOT include any extra header/footer lines.

    3. After creating the file, also echo the same ordered lines to STDOUT
       so that someone running the commands in the terminal immediately sees
       the result.

    A quick sanity check for you: the input list is very small (fewer than a
    dozen lines) and therefore only 3–4 shell commands should be enough
    (e.g. one pipeline plus one redirection).

    The automated tests will:
      • Verify that /home/user/releases/service_frequency.log exists.  
      • Confirm the exact line-by-line content and ordering described above.  
      • Ensure the file is world-readable (mode 644 or more permissive).  
      • Capture your terminal STDOUT during execution and check that it is
        byte-for-byte identical to the file’s contents (trailing new-line
        allowed).

    Hint: remember that “uniq” only collapses adjacent identical lines, so
    you must sort the input first.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing input file before the agent starts -->
    File: /home/user/releases/services_2023-10.txt
    Mode: 644
    Contents (9 lines, LF line endings):
        auth
        billing
        auth
        reports
        auth
        billing
        search
        search
        analytics

    <!-- Expected artefact after the agent finishes -->
    File: /home/user/releases/service_frequency.log
    Mode: 644
    Exact contents (5 lines, LF line endings):
        auth: 3
        billing: 2
        search: 2
        analytics: 1
        reports: 1
name: task_000000_0b284f8e
